title: What It Means to Steward Wealth
subtitle: A journal prompt set for exploring the identity of wealth steward and naming the values that guide how money moves through you
excerpt: A four-prompt journal set from Day 76 of a 12-week money programme. The prompts move from exploring what stewardship means, toward naming the values that guide how wealth moves, to finding the specific moments where you have already used money with intention.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 76 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to get clear on the values that guide your wealth decisions, or when the steward identity feels theoretical rather than lived.

content.text: A four-prompt journal set from Day 76 of a 12-week money programme. Day 76 theme: celebrating the identity of wealth steward. The prompts move from exploring what stewardship means to you, toward naming the values that guide how wealth moves, to finding the specific recent moments where you have already used money with intention. Set a timer. Write without editing.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
What does it mean to you to be a steward of wealth rather than an owner of it?
Write your own definition. What is the difference between owning something and holding it with purpose?
How does it feel to view money as something that moves through you, rather than something that belongs to you?
Write what changes in the picture when money is a flow rather than a stock. What does it ask of you?
What values will guide how you use your wealth for good?
Name three. Write what each one looks like as a specific decision, not just as an idea.
What recent opportunity have you had to use money with love, integrity, or generosity?
Write the specific moment. What did you do? What did it feel like to direct wealth with intention?
Adapted from the Day 76 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
